texts.label: text
texts.orig: Synthetic  15-page  PDF  with  paragraphs,  simple  and  complex  tables,  diagrams,  scanned-form  style  image,  metadata examples, and production RAG edge cases.
texts.text: Synthetic  15-page  PDF  with  paragraphs,  simple  and  complex  tables,  diagrams,  scanned-form  style  image,  metadata examples, and production RAG edge cases.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Three client teams - Arka Finance, BlueLeaf Retail, and CityRide Mobility - are migrating contracts, policies, support records, and  operational  reports  into  a  single  RAG  platform.  Each  team  has  different  document  types,  access  rules,  and  parsing challenges. The RAG system must answer questions with citations while ensuring that one client never sees another clients data.
texts.text: Three client teams - Arka Finance, BlueLeaf Retail, and CityRide Mobility - are migrating contracts, policies, support records, and  operational  reports  into  a  single  RAG  platform.  Each  team  has  different  document  types,  access  rules,  and  parsing challenges. The RAG system must answer questions with citations while ensuring that one client never sees another clients data.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This  PDF  is  intentionally  designed  to  test  document  loaders,  PDF  parsers,  OCR  workflows,  table  extraction,  chunking strategies, metadata preservation, and source citation quality.
texts.text: This  PDF  is  intentionally  designed  to  test  document  loaders,  PDF  parsers,  OCR  workflows,  table  extraction,  chunking strategies, metadata preservation, and source citation quality.

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Key statement: RAG does not train the model. RAG gives the model the right context before answering.
texts.text: Key statement: RAG does not train the model. RAG gives the model the right context before answering.

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Complex RAG Parsing Sample - synthetic document
texts.text: Complex RAG Parsing Sample - synthetic document

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The platform team is building a document intelligence layer for contract operations. Legal users need answers such as: Which contracts  renew  automatically?  Which  agreements  have  a  60-day  termination  notice?  Which  documents  mention  data residency in India? These questions require retrieval across multiple document types and sometimes across multiple related documents of a single client.
texts.text: The platform team is building a document intelligence layer for contract operations. Legal users need answers such as: Which contracts  renew  automatically?  Which  agreements  have  a  60-day  termination  notice?  Which  documents  mention  data residency in India? These questions require retrieval across multiple document types and sometimes across multiple related documents of a single client.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Support teams also want answers from SOPs, markdown runbooks, HTML exports, chat transcripts, and CSV logs. A single parser is not enough because the corpus contains born-digital PDFs, scanned forms, HTML, markdown, and spreadsheet-like tables.
texts.text: Support teams also want answers from SOPs, markdown runbooks, HTML exports, chat transcripts, and CSV logs. A single parser is not enough because the corpus contains born-digital PDFs, scanned forms, HTML, markdown, and spreadsheet-like tables.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Caption: Ownership map showing how client admins, legal teams, audit teams, document storage, vector storage, and the RAG service interact.
texts.text: Caption: Ownership map showing how client admins, legal teams, audit teams, document storage, vector storage, and the RAG service interact.

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Complex RAG Parsing Sample - synthetic document
texts.text: Complex RAG Parsing Sample - synthetic document

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The ingestion team receives files from multiple sources. Some files are clean and digital; others contain scanned pages, rotated tables, missing metadata, embedded images, or formula-generated values. The inventory below intentionally includes different formats and extraction expectations.
texts.text: The ingestion team receives files from multiple sources. Some files are clean and digital; others contain scanned pages, rotated tables, missing metadata, embedded images, or formula-generated values. The inventory below intentionally includes different formats and extraction expectations.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Parsing difficulty increases when a document combines multiple layouts: paragraph text, tables, images, and notes. Good parsers preserve structure; weak parsers flatten everything into a noisy text stream.
texts.text: Parsing difficulty increases when a document combines multiple layouts: paragraph text, tables, images, and notes. Good parsers preserve structure; weak parsers flatten everything into a noisy text stream.

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Complex RAG Parsing Sample - synthetic document
texts.text: Complex RAG Parsing Sample - synthetic document

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Clause 7.2  -  Confidential  Information.  The  Receiving  Party  shall  protect  Confidential  Information  using  at  least  the  same degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential materials, but in no event less than reasonable care. Confidential Information includes technical documents, pricing schedules, user lists, API keys, operational procedures, security reports, incident response notes, product roadmaps, and any derived analysis prepared by the Receiving Party.
texts.text: Clause 7.2  -  Confidential  Information.  The  Receiving  Party  shall  protect  Confidential  Information  using  at  least  the  same degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential materials, but in no event less than reasonable care. Confidential Information includes technical documents, pricing schedules, user lists, API keys, operational procedures, security reports, incident response notes, product roadmaps, and any derived analysis prepared by the Receiving Party.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Clause  7.3  -  Exclusions.  Confidential  Information  does  not  include  information  that  is  publicly  available  without  breach, independently developed without reference to the Disclosing Party material, received lawfully from a third party, or approved for release in writing. The burden of proving an exclusion remains with the Receiving Party.
texts.text: Clause  7.3  -  Exclusions.  Confidential  Information  does  not  include  information  that  is  publicly  available  without  breach, independently developed without reference to the Disclosing Party material, received lawfully from a third party, or approved for release in writing. The burden of proving an exclusion remains with the Receiving Party.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Clause 9.1  -  Data  Residency.  If  the  Statement  of  Work  identifies  a  specific  processing  region,  the  service  provider  shall process production records only within that region unless the client approves a transfer in writing. Disaster recovery replicas may be stored in a secondary region if encryption at rest and access logging are enabled.
texts.text: Clause 9.1  -  Data  Residency.  If  the  Statement  of  Work  identifies  a  specific  processing  region,  the  service  provider  shall process production records only within that region unless the client approves a transfer in writing. Disaster recovery replicas may be stored in a secondary region if encryption at rest and access logging are enabled.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Clause 11.4 - Termination Assistance. Upon termination, the provider shall make client data available for export for a period of thirty days. After the export period, the provider may delete production data according to the deletion schedule unless a legal hold applies.
texts.text: Clause 11.4 - Termination Assistance. Upon termination, the provider shall make client data available for export for a period of thirty days. After the export period, the provider may delete production data according to the deletion schedule unless a legal hold applies.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Parser note: clause numbering is important. Chunking should not remove or split clause identifiers because users often ask questions using clause numbers.
texts.text: Parser note: clause numbering is important. Chunking should not remove or split clause identifiers because users often ask questions using clause numbers.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The following table is intentionally simple. It should be correctly extracted by most PDF table parsers. It tests basic row and column detection, short text values, and numeric values.
texts.text: The following table is intentionally simple. It should be correctly extracted by most PDF table parsers. It tests basic row and column detection, short text values, and numeric values.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This  table  appears  between  paragraphs,  which  is  common  in  enterprise  documents.  A  good  parser  should  preserve  the paragraph before the table, the table content, and the paragraph after the table in the correct reading order.
texts.text: This  table  appears  between  paragraphs,  which  is  common  in  enterprise  documents.  A  good  parser  should  preserve  the paragraph before the table, the table content, and the paragraph after the table in the correct reading order.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For RAG systems, simple tables are often converted into row-wise text chunks such as: Policy Area: Refunds; Rule: Refund requests must be raised within 7 days; Owner: Customer Support.
texts.text: For RAG systems, simple tables are often converted into row-wise text chunks such as: Policy Area: Refunds; Rule: Refund requests must be raised within 7 days; Owner: Customer Support.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5. Complex Table: SLA, Escalation, and Credits
texts.text: 5. Complex Table: SLA, Escalation, and Credits

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This table uses long cells and operational footnotes. It is designed to test whether the parser can retain header hierarchy and map values to the correct columns.
texts.text: This table uses long cells and operational footnotes. It is designed to test whether the parser can retain header hierarchy and map values to the correct columns.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Footnote A: Service credits are not cumulative across the same service category for the same calendar month. Footnote B: Credits do not apply when delay is caused by client-side network restrictions, missing credentials, or force majeure events.
texts.text: Footnote A: Service credits are not cumulative across the same service category for the same calendar month. Footnote B: Credits do not apply when delay is caused by client-side network restrictions, missing credentials, or force majeure events.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Parser note: table footnotes should remain attached to the table. If the footnote is separated, an answer about credits may become incomplete or misleading.
texts.text: Parser note: table footnotes should remain attached to the table. If the footnote is separated, an answer about credits may become incomplete or misleading.

texts.text: The diagram below represents the ingestion and retrieval flow. Some PDF parsers ignore images completely, while others extract image metadata but not the text inside the image. For production use, image content may require OCR or multimodal extraction.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Markdown  files  are  often  easier  to  parse  because  headings  and  code  blocks  are  explicit.  However,  when  markdown  is exported to PDF, the structure may become visual rather than semantic.
texts.text: Markdown  files  are  often  easier  to  parse  because  headings  and  code  blocks  are  explicit.  However,  when  markdown  is exported to PDF, the structure may become visual rather than semantic.

texts.label: code
texts.orig: # Runbook: Contract Ingestion Failure ## Symptoms - Upload status remains `processing` for more than 30 minutes. - Worker logs show repeated timeout errors. - Vector count does not increase for the affected document. ## Recovery Steps 1. Confirm the object exists in document storage. 2. Re-run parser with `safe_mode=True`. 3. Rebuild chunks with the previous chunking configuration. 4. Compare chunk count with the last successful ingestion. 5. Trigger re-embedding only for changed chunks.
texts.text: # Runbook: Contract Ingestion Failure ## Symptoms - Upload status remains `processing` for more than 30 minutes. - Worker logs show repeated timeout errors. - Vector count does not increase for the affected document. ## Recovery Steps 1. Confirm the object exists in document storage. 2. Re-run parser with `safe_mode=True`. 3. Rebuild chunks with the previous chunking configuration. 4. Compare chunk count with the last successful ingestion. 5. Trigger re-embedding only for changed chunks.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A good parser should preserve code block boundaries and avoid mixing numbered steps with surrounding prose. In RAG, runbooks are useful because users often ask operational questions that require exact steps.
texts.text: A good parser should preserve code block boundaries and avoid mixing numbered steps with surrounding prose. In RAG, runbooks are useful because users often ask operational questions that require exact steps.

texts.orig: This page contains a scanned-form style image. Text inside the form is not normal selectable PDF text. A simple text parser may miss it entirely. OCR-based systems should extract labels, values, and checkboxes from the image.
texts.text: This page contains a scanned-form style image. Text inside the form is not normal selectable PDF text. A simple text parser may miss it entirely. OCR-based systems should extract labels, values, and checkboxes from the image.

texts.text: Approved for ingestion after Pll masking

texts.label: text
texts.orig: BlueLeaf  Retail  has  five  related  contract  documents.  If  these  documents  are  indexed  independently  without  relationship metadata,  the  retriever  may  miss  cross-document  context.  The  platform  uses  a  contract_group_id  to  connect  related documents.
texts.text: BlueLeaf  Retail  has  five  related  contract  documents.  If  these  documents  are  indexed  independently  without  relationship metadata,  the  retriever  may  miss  cross-document  context.  The  platform  uses  a  contract_group_id  to  connect  related documents.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Example  question:  What  does  the  BlueLeaf  agreement  say  about  termination  assistance  and  pricing  changes?  A  good retriever may need chunks from the MSA and the Pricing Amendment together.
texts.text: Example  question:  What  does  the  BlueLeaf  agreement  say  about  termination  assistance  and  pricing  changes?  A  good retriever may need chunks from the MSA and the Pricing Amendment together.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Relationship metadata allows retrieval across related documents without mixing unrelated client data.
texts.text: Relationship metadata allows retrieval across related documents without mixing unrelated client data.

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Complex RAG Parsing Sample - synthetic document
texts.text: Complex RAG Parsing Sample - synthetic document

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In a multi-tenant RAG system, each document, chunk, embedding, and retrieval request should be associated with a tenant identifier. Client isolation should happen before the LLM receives any context.
texts.text: In a multi-tenant RAG system, each document, chunk, embedding, and retrieval request should be associated with a tenant identifier. Client isolation should happen before the LLM receives any context.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Correct flow: authenticate user, get client_id from backend, retrieve only matching chunks, build prompt with authorized context, return answer with citations.
texts.text: Correct flow: authenticate user, get client_id from backend, retrieve only matching chunks, build prompt with authorized context, return answer with citations.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Wrong flow: retrieve from all clients and tell the model to ignore unauthorized content. This is unsafe because the LLM should not be used as the access-control boundary.
texts.text: Wrong flow: retrieve from all clients and tell the model to ignore unauthorized content. This is unsafe because the LLM should not be used as the access-control boundary.

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Complex RAG Parsing Sample - synthetic document
texts.text: Complex RAG Parsing Sample - synthetic document

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The heatmap below simulates viewership retention by time slot. A parser that ignores chart images will miss useful business context. OCR can extract axis labels and numbers, but chart understanding may require a vision model.
texts.text: The heatmap below simulates viewership retention by time slot. A parser that ignores chart images will miss useful business context. OCR can extract axis labels and numbers, but chart understanding may require a vision model.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Viewer Retention Heatmap by Time Slot
texts.text: Viewer Retention Heatmap by Time Slot

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For RAG, chart captions should be indexed. If chart values are critical, extract them into structured metadata or a companion table.
texts.text: For RAG, chart captions should be indexed. If chart values are critical, extract them into structured metadata or a companion table.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A  RAG  system  should  be  evaluated  separately  for  retrieval  quality  and  answer  quality.  This  page  includes  a  miniature evaluation set with expected source references. It is useful for testing whether citations point to the correct section.
texts.text: A  RAG  system  should  be  evaluated  separately  for  retrieval  quality  and  answer  quality.  This  page  includes  a  miniature evaluation set with expected source references. It is useful for testing whether citations point to the correct section.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Evaluation should include adversarial questions, unrelated questions, and questions that require multi-hop retrieval across related documents.
texts.text: Evaluation should include adversarial questions, unrelated questions, and questions that require multi-hop retrieval across related documents.

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Complex RAG Parsing Sample - synthetic document
texts.text: Complex RAG Parsing Sample - synthetic document

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The following cases often break real document ingestion pipelines. They are included here as guidance for testing parser quality before moving to embeddings and vector storage.
texts.text: The following cases often break real document ingestion pipelines. They are included here as guidance for testing parser quality before moving to embeddings and vector storage.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Bad parsing creates bad chunks. Bad chunks create bad retrieval. Bad retrieval creates bad answers.
texts.text: Bad parsing creates bad chunks. Bad chunks create bad retrieval. Bad retrieval creates bad answers.

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Complex RAG Parsing Sample - synthetic document
texts.text: Complex RAG Parsing Sample - synthetic document

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Use this checklist before sending parsed content into chunking and embeddings. It helps identify whether the data is ready for production RAG.
texts.text: Use this checklist before sending parsed content into chunking and embeddings. It helps identify whether the data is ready for production RAG.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Summary: RAG is about knowledge access. Fine-tuning is about behavior adaptation. For document intelligence, parsing quality is the foundation. If extraction is weak, no embedding model or LLM can fully fix the missing context.
texts.text: Summary: RAG is about knowledge access. Fine-tuning is about behavior adaptation. For document intelligence, parsing quality is the foundation. If extraction is weak, no embedding model or LLM can fully fix the missing context.
